---
title: "Post Template: Formulas, Code, and Images"
description: "A reference template showing how to use math, code blocks, images, and callouts in your blog."
date: "2026-01-01"
categories: [Template]
draft: true          # ← Set to false when ready to publish
toc: true
---

> This is a **draft template** — set `draft: false` in the front matter to publish it.

## Inline and Block Math

Inline formula: the attention score is $a_{ij} = \text{softmax}\!\left(\frac{q_i k_j^T}{\sqrt{d_k}}\right)$.

Block formula (KaTeX renders it beautifully):

$$
\text{Attention}(Q, K, V) = \text{softmax}\!\left(\frac{QK^T}{\sqrt{d_k}}\right)V
$$

Multi-line with alignment:

$$
\begin{aligned}
\mu &= \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^N x_i \\
\sigma^2 &= \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^N (x_i - \mu)^2
\end{aligned}
$$

## Code Blocks

Python with syntax highlighting:

```python
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F

def scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v, mask=None):
    d_k = q.size(-1)
    scores = torch.matmul(q, k.transpose(-2, -1)) / d_k ** 0.5
    if mask is not None:
        scores = scores.masked_fill(mask == 0, float('-inf'))
    weights = F.softmax(scores, dim=-1)
    return torch.matmul(weights, v)
```

CUDA C++:

```cuda
__global__ void softmax_kernel(float* x, float* out, int N) {
    int i = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (i < N) {
        // ... reduction over shared memory
    }
}
```

## Images

```markdown
![Caption for your image](images/your_image.png){width=80%}
```

Or with a figure number:

```markdown
::: {#fig-arch}
![](images/architecture.png)

Figure: Transformer architecture overview.
:::
```

## Callouts

::: {.callout-tip}
Use callouts to highlight key insights.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
Warn readers about common gotchas.
:::

::: {.callout-note}
Add extra context that doesn't fit in the main flow.
:::

## Tables

| Method | Memory (GB) | Throughput (tok/s) |
|---|---|---|
| FP32 | 28 | 120 |
| BF16 | 14 | 240 |
| INT8 | 7 | 380 |

## Jupyter Notebook Posts

To use a `.ipynb` as a blog post directly, place it in a post folder:

```
posts/
  my-notebook-post/
    index.ipynb        ← Quarto renders this automatically
    images/
```

The notebook's cells become the post. Add this to the first cell (Raw type):

```yaml
---
title: "My Notebook Post"
date: "2026-01-01"
categories: [CUDA, Python]
---
```
